title: Case/Care Management * Older Adults - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Case/Care Management * Older Adults 58 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Chronic/Severe Mental Illness Deafness Disabilities Issues Hard of Hearing Older Adults Hispanic/Latino Community Immigrants Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Homeless Veterans Veterans Native American Community AIDS/HIV Issues AVANTI ELDERCARE RESOURCES INC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides consultations and assessments for care and housing needs, evaluates financial and insurance resources, household and care management, monitoring/oversight, money management, and guardian and conservator services. Facilitates communication with health care providers, provides patient accompaniment to medical appointments, and home visits available for assessments and bill paying. "Just in Case" plan and estate-planning services also available. 911 Country Club Road Suite 250 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older with cognitive impairments, mental health challenges, brain injury, persons with complex health conditions, those who have been subject to financial or emotional abuse of an adult or elder, and persons who need help managing financial or health-care matters. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm or by appointment On call 24 hours per day / 7 days per week for urgent needs of established clients Email (541) 344-7712 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES OPTIONS COUNSELING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers counselors to help individuals make informed long-term care decisions and determine their strengths, needs, preferences, and unique situations. Provides information about support strategies, action plans, and tactics based on the choices available in the community. Options Counseling can be a stand-alone service for a participant or can lead to Older Americans Act Case Management (OAA). OAA is a service for individuals who need ongoing support to remain independent. Care coordinators assist in problem-solving, advocate, and connect participants to resources and community support to enhance the quality of life and future planning for later years. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Options Counseling: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, veterans, family members, or caregivers OAA: Age 60 and older Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm (503) 655-8640 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON HERMISTON OPTIONS COUNSELING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Options counselors assist individuals in making informed decisions about long-term care. Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE VALLEY COUNCIL OF GOVERNMENTS OPTIONS COUNSELING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Options counselors help individuals make informed long-term care decisions. Counselors help individuals determine their strengths, needs, preferences, and unique situations. Provides information about support strategies, action plans, and tactics based on the choices available in the community. Options Counseling can be a stand-alone service for a participant or can lead to Older Americans Act Case Management (OAA). OAA is a service for individuals who need ongoing support to remain independent. Care coordinators assist in problem-solving, advocate, and connect participants to resources and community support to enhance the quality of life and future planning for later years. 155 N 1st Street Central Point, OR 97502 Eligibility: -Options Counseling: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, veterans, and their family members or caregivers -OAA: Age 60 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 618-7572 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH COAST BUSINESS MAIN OFFICE OPTIONS COUNSELING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Options counselors assist individuals in making informed decisions about long-term care.
